eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue incremental advances along the Donetsk Oblast frontline, with marginal gains reported near Novooleksandrivka, Predtechyne, and Horykhove in the past week, per OSINT sources like DeepState, but face fierce Ukrainian resistance around fortified hubs including Pokrovsk, Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, and Kostiantynivka. These slow, costly pushes reflect a grinding war of attrition, where Russia controls roughly three-quarters of the oblast but holds none of its administrative center or major western cities after four years of fighting. A three-day ceasefire announcement by former U.S. President Trump on May 8 offers brief de-escalation, though mutual strikes persist; upcoming diplomatic talks and seasonal offensives could sway momentum in this protracted territorial contest.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices United Russia at 95.8% implied probability to secure the most seats in the September 18–20 State Duma election, driven by its incumbency as the ruling party backed by President Putin, consistent poll leads of 35–39% in late April FOM surveys for party-list proportional representation, and expected dominance in 225 single-member districts through organizational strength. Opposition parties like Communist Party (KPRF at 8–10%), LDPR (10–11%), and New People (6%) trail amid internal challenges, limited grassroots, and Kremlin-managed competition, as detailed in recent analyses of campaign preparations. No major shifts in the past 30 days, with stable ratings despite high undecideds; extraordinary scenarios like acute economic distress, elite infighting, or Ukraine war setbacks could challenge this, though structural barriers remain formidable.

Spaun lurking at 11-under. Pre-tournament withdrawals by Scottie Scheffler and Collin Morikawa to prep for the PGA Championship thinned the elite field, enabling surges like Fowler's bogey-free 63 in Round 2 and Fitzpatrick's 64 in Round 3. Earlier weather delays disrupted opening rounds, but improving conditions favor low scores; top 20 markets hinge on final-round stability amid Quail Hollow's brutal Green Mile (holes 16-18), where course history and putting prowess separate contenders from fades.

markets.question: Another GTA VI trailer released by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if another official trailer for the next GTA (GTA VI) is released by Rockstar Games or any of its official accounts, including but not limited to Rockstar's official website, YouTube channel, Twitter, or any other official social media platform between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An "official trailer" is defined as a video clearly labelled and marketed as a trailer for the game in question. Teasers, gameplay snippets, or other promotional materials will not qualify for resolution unless explicitly marketed as an official trailer by Rockstar Games.

This market will resolve immediately if such an official trailer is released by the expiry date. If no official trailer is released by the specified date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "Yes" for another GTA VI trailer by May 31 at 70.5% implied probability, driven by Rockstar Games' historical pattern of ramping up marketing six months ahead of launch—now set for November 19, 2026, following the November 2025 delay announcement. markets.question: Will Mahmoud Ahmadinejad be head of state in Iran end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Iran on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Iran’s territory at the specified time.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mojtaba Khamenei's appointment as Supreme Leader in March 2026 by the Assembly of Experts, following Ali Khamenei's assassination in US-Israeli strikes, anchors trader consensus at 67% for his continued tenure through year-end, bolstered by IRGC backing and regime continuity amid wartime conditions. Recent health rumors from injuries sustained in strikes or assassination attempts have eased with President Pezeshkian's May 7 meeting confirming his stability and Hassan Khomeini's May 10 statement affirming full control over state affairs. Reza Pahlavi's 8.5% reflects diaspora hopes for upheaval, but absent major unrest or no-confidence challenges, hardliners favor dynastic succession despite clerical unease over credentials. Upcoming policy deadlines and escalation risks could test this positioning.

markets.question: Will "Lord of the Flies" be the #2 global Netflix show this week?

Man on Fire's explosive debut as Chernin Entertainment's record-breaking series has fueled its edge, while Should I Marry A Murderer?'s true-crime docuseries grip sustains momentum; Legends, fresh off its May 7 premiere, gains from early buzz in a crowded May slate including Lord of the Flies and The Boroughs. Weekend binge surges and international viewer shifts could upend rankings before Tuesday's final tally, underscoring streaming's volatility.

markets.question: Will Trump say "NVIDIA" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 11, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump dance on May 10, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00 AM ET and 11:59 PM ET on the specified date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media, which were filmed outside this market's time frame, will not qualify.

This market will resolve based on video footage.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 86.5% on US acquisition of part of Greenland in 2026, driven by firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections of sovereignty transfer amid insurmountable legal barriers under international law, including self-determination principles and the Kingdom of Denmark's authority. Early-year tensions peaked with President Trump's tariff threats against opposing NATO allies and "hard way" rhetoric, but de-escalated at the January 21 Davos summit via a non-binding "framework" for Arctic security cooperation—expanding Thule Air Base access without territorial cession. Recent May approvals for US firm Critical Metals' 70% stake in a rare-earth mining project signal economic partnerships over annexation, with no official negotiations or congressional momentum on bills like the Greenland Annexation Act, underscoring slim odds absent a geopolitical crisis.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue leads trader consensus at 81% implied probability for the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary on September 1 following his landslide endorsement win—around 70%—at the state GOP convention on April 26, where he secured ballot qualification and party backing as a former Abiomed CEO and major donor. Brian Shortsleeve qualified for the ballot at 13% trader odds, pressing for debates to contrast records on affordability and housing amid calls from some quarters to consolidate behind Minogue and avoid a divisive primary. Mike Kennealy suspended his campaign after failing the 15% threshold, dropping his odds to 0.7% as the field narrows to two ahead of voter turnout in this blue-leaning state.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution over a permanent Attorney General nomination by June 30, more than five weeks after President Trump's April 2 dismissal of Pam Bondi, with loyalist Todd Blanche installed as acting Attorney General—able to serve up to 210 days under the Federal Vacancies Reform Act amid his aggressive pursuit of administration priorities like targeting political adversaries. EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin leads speculative odds following reports positioning him atop Trump's shortlist, though he recently dodged direct questions on the role during interviews. Harmeet Dhillon and Blanche trail closely, buoyed by their legal ties to Trump, while Texas AG Ken Paxton's probabilities rose after his April 27 Supreme Court win defending the state's electoral map; no formal announcement or Senate confirmation process has advanced, underscoring an unhurried timeline.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections, which produced a hung National Assembly requiring 46 seats for a majority, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement (29 seats) failed to secure a coalition and shifted to opposition on April 20. Center-right parties—Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS, 28 seats, led by Janez Janša), NSi-SLS-FOKUS alliance (9 seats), and Democrats (6 seats)—hold 43 seats collectively, positioning them to form a minority government with potential external support from Resni.ca (5 seats). On May 5, Janša announced agreement on core coalition guidelines, with details expected soon. President Nataša Pirc Musar has urged negotiations after declining an initial prime minister nominee; traders eye her upcoming mandate and the confidence vote amid deadlock risks or snap elections.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Turkey" or "Turkiye" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's verbal mentions of animals in public speeches drive this Polymarket, resolving Yes for recorded audio/video references to specific terms from May 1-31, 2026; written posts like Truth Social do not qualify. On May 1, he referenced "shark" in keeping with his recurring anecdotes, followed by extensive "dog" discussion and a "bear" mention—recalling his cognitive test—during a May 2 speech at the Forum Club of the Palm Beaches critiquing White House pet traditions. No additional animals noted through May 10 amid his presidential schedule. Traders weigh his unscripted rhetorical style, prone to animal analogies in rallies and addresses, against the remaining three weeks; upcoming public events like potential speeches or White House appearances could shift probabilities before month-end resolution.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 73% implied probability against the U.S. enacting an AI safety bill before 2027, stemming from stalled federal legislative progress and partisan gridlock on regulatory scope. The Trump administration's March 2026 National Policy Framework emphasizes innovation-friendly guardrails, proposing preemption of emerging state AI laws without establishing new federal oversight bodies or stringent safety mandates, signaling a light-touch approach over comprehensive regulation. No major AI safety bills have advanced through Congress this year despite introductions on issues like child protections and data centers, overshadowed by appropriations battles and midterm election dynamics. While a post-November lame-duck session provides a narrow window, competing priorities and historical filibuster hurdles sustain trader skepticism.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Australia’s Delta Goodrem with “Eclipse” as the narrow jury frontrunner at 25.5% implied probability, buoyed by her established vocal prowess and the ballad’s sophisticated arrangement that echoes past jury favorites like ballads from Loreen or Emmelie de Forest. France’s young internal selectee Monroe follows closely with “Regarde!” at 23.5%, praised in pre-rehearsal previews for its emotive delivery and French chanson elegance, a perennial jury magnet. Denmark’s Søren Torpegaard Lund (“Før vi går hjem”), fresh off a dominant Dansk Melodi Grand Prix win, and Finland’s duo Linda Lampenius x Pete Parkkonen (“Liekinheitin”) round out the top four at 21% and 19%, their folk-infused pop blending broad appeal with compositional depth. With first rehearsals underway in Vienna ahead of semis on May 12 and 14, staging execution and live vocal stability will be pivotal swing factors in this neck-and-neck jury race, where no entry exceeds 30% amid high uncertainty.

markets.question: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Millwall FC and Hull City AFC, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Millwall FC and Hull City AFC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the EFL Championship play-off semi-final second leg on May 11 at The Den, 3rd-placed Millwall host 6th-placed Hull City with promotion to the Premier League at stake, following the first leg at the MKM Stadium on May 8. Millwall's 3-1 away victory over Hull on March 7—thanks to goals from Jake Cooper, Macaulay Ivanović, and Josh Coburn—highlights their recent head-to-head edge and strong away form late in the season. Injury concerns persist for both: Hull sidelined by Eliot Matazo's ACL tear and Cody Drameh's absence, while Millwall monitors Josh Coburn's fitness and Caleb Taylor's foot issue. Millwall's higher finish grants home advantage in this decisive leg, amid high-stakes tension and packed schedules.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between Gaziantep FK and Rams Başakşehir FK.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin Süper Lig contest at Gaziantep Stadium, with draw priced at 51.5% amid Gaziantep FK's home edge offsetting İstanbul Başakşehir FK's stronger table position (6th vs. 11th) and superior recent form. Gaziantep slumped to a third straight defeat, 2-1 at Göztepe last weekend, scoring sparingly while grappling with injuries to forward Yusuf Kabadayi and defender Salem M'Bakata (cruciate ligament tear), alongside earlier absences like Mustafa Burak Bozan. Başakşehir arrives buoyant from a 3-0 home clean sheet over Samsunspor and 12 goals across their last five outings (three wins, one draw), but their away record tempers favoritism. Head-to-head history favors the visitors (10 wins to four), yet these mid-table clashes frequently stalemate, bunching probabilities tightly around 50% apiece.

events.title: AC Milan vs. Atalanta BC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Serie A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between AC Milan and Atalanta BC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atalanta BC's 98% implied probability stems from their commanding 3-0 lead over AC Milan in the 65th minute of this Serie A Round 36 clash at San Siro, with goals from Éderson (7'), Zappacosta (29'), and Raspadori (51') exploiting Milan's defensive frailties. Pre-match, Milan entered depleted by Fikayo Tomori's suspension from a prior red card, season-ending facial injury to Luka Modrić, and Christian Pulisic's muscular issue, amid a dismal run of one goal in five games and recent losses threatening top-four hopes. Atalanta, unbeaten in their last six head-to-heads including four wins, capitalized early. A Milan comeback would require four unanswered goals—improbable given their blunt attack—though an Atalanta red card or collapse could shift dynamics late.

markets.question: Will Roy Barreras win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices unidentified moderate Candidate M a slim 49.5% implied probability to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election, with right-wing outsider Abelardo de la Espriella close at 42% and leftist Iván Cepeda Castro at 38.5%, reflecting a fragmented first-round field just 21 days before the May 31 vote and uncertain runoff dynamics on June 21. Recent polls like Invamer (late April) show Cepeda leading at 44% on Pacto Histórico continuity amid Petro's term limit, but AtlasIntel tracking indicates narrowing gaps with de la Espriella surging to 32% on security pledges and Paloma Valencia at 20% post-center-right primary win. The race stays tight due to no candidate polling over 50%, potential anti-left vote consolidation, and volatility from undecideds; separation could come from debates, endorsements, or violence spikes in key regions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Helsinki be 14°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fi/vantaa/EFHK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Helsinki's May 11 high temperature splits closely between 15°C (38.5%) and 14°C (32.5%), reflecting divergent short-range forecasts from the Finnish Meteorological Institute (FMI) and global models like ECMWF and GFS, which project peaks of 12-15°C amid overcast skies and light 3-6 m/s winds. This aligns with mid-May climatology at Helsinki-Vantaa Airport, where average highs hit 14.4°C, but current cooling after May 10's 15°C peak—driven by persistent cloud cover—tips odds cooler, with lower outcomes accounting for rare outbreaks. Key differentiators include afternoon clearing potential boosting 15-16°C versus sustained clouds favoring 14°C or below; FMI updates overnight could shift ensembles further. Resolution hinges on official Helsinki-Vantaa observations.

Recent Q1 finance reports and a combative April 24 debate among the top three intensified scrutiny, with El-Sayed's ballot access secured via signatures. The August 4 primary remains wide open, hinging on turnout in this open-seat race to succeed retiring Sen. Gary Peters.

High-profile withdrawals last week—including Scottie Scheffler resting for the PGA Championship, Collin Morikawa nursing a back injury, Jake Knapp sidelined by thumb issues, Jason Day, and Billy Horschel post-surgery—thinned the elite field, boosting mid-range contenders like Fowler, whose second-round 63 sparked his surge. The renovated par-71 layout demands elite ball-striking and precision on the tough "Green Mile" closing stretch (holes 16-18), with final-round pressure and minimal rest advantages shaping trader consensus on podium locks versus longshot top-5 bids.

Njoku, who announced via Instagram in February that he won't return to the Cleveland Browns after nine seasons—ending at just 24.5%—remains unsigned post-2026 NFL Draft, fueling interest from TE-needy teams like the Washington Commanders (48.9%), Philadelphia Eagles (48.8%), and Cincinnati Bengals (48.5%) based on roster gaps and prior rumors, though no other visits have matched the Chargers' momentum.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Certified final results from New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, released around May 4 following canvass completion, show Democrat Analilia Mejia defeating Republican Joe Hathaway 60.2% to 39.4%—a 20.8 percentage point margin—driving trader consensus to price Mejia's 20-25% victory bucket at 97.5%. This outcome in the safely Democratic suburban North Jersey seat, vacated by Gov. Mikie Sherrill, exceeded Kamala Harris's 2024 presidential margin there by over 11 points and prior performances by Sherrill, reflecting strong Democratic turnout on April 16 despite low special election participation. With all votes counted and no significant provisional ballots outstanding, scenarios like a recount or legal challenge are improbable absent extraordinary evidence, locking in market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie's overwhelming domestic box office dominance, with a cumulative $407 million through early May after a record-shattering $190 million five-day opening and steady family-audience legs across five weeks, underpins trader consensus at 99.8% implied probability for highest-grossing April release on May 31. Michael, the Jaafar Jackson-led biopic, surged to a $97 million debut on April 25 but has frontloaded sharply—second-weekend drop exceeding 50%—reaching only around $212 million domestically amid mixed critical reception despite fan enthusiasm. Lesser contenders like Mother Mary ($2.5 million), You, Me & Tuscany ($18 million), and indies such as Wasteland Cop or Lorne trail far behind. An upset would require Michael to outpace Mario's projected Memorial Day hold by over $200 million in three weeks, a near-impossible feat given historical biopic trajectories and Mario's Nintendo-fueled momentum.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Peng" during events with Xi Jinping?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping from May 14 to May 15, 2026 (https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/04/china/china-us-talks-iran-intl-hnk). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: **President Donald Trump's bilateral summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026, drives this market's focus, with U.S. advance teams arriving via C-17 last week to prepare high-security logistics.** The White House confirmed the visit proceeds amid Iran ceasefire talks, where Trump noted Xi's satisfaction with the reopened Strait of Hormuz easing Chinese oil imports. Trump's April comments called the encounter "potentially historic," anticipating advances on trade tariffs, technology restrictions, and regional issues like Taiwan—following Xi's April meeting with Taiwan's opposition leader invoking reunification threats. Traders assess Trump's negotiation style, shaped by prior U.S.-China diplomacy, against Beijing's leverage from recent sanctions and economic pressures, with outcomes hinging on his public statements during the event.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The district’s R+8 Cook Partisan Voter Index and Grothman’s history of double-digit general election margins—61% in 2024—bolster this positioning, amid a fragmented Democratic primary featuring eight candidates, none with significant fundraising to challenge his $700,000 cash on hand as of late March. Recent FEC filings highlight Bradley Smith’s modest grassroots momentum among Democrats, but no polls exist yet in this early cycle ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Independents like firefighter Michael Thurow add minor uncertainty, though structural advantages favor Republicans absent major shifts.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $296 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon's Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 showcased robust growth, with net sales surging 17% year-over-year to $181.5 billion—beating estimates of $177.3 billion—and operating income hitting a record $23.9 billion at 13.1% margins, fueled by AWS revenue up 28% to $37.6 billion amid AI demand. Shares traded near recent highs around $272-278 following the release, reflecting trader consensus on sustained e-commerce recovery and cloud dominance, with analyst price targets averaging $313 (up to $370). Implied probabilities price in modest upside by month-end, ahead of the May 20 annual shareholder meeting; broader market volatility and Q2 guidance previews loom as key swing factors.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's 16 qualified powerhouses including world No. 1 France, No. 2 Spain, England, Portugal, and the Netherlands, confirmed after dramatic March playoffs where Bosnia and Herzegovina ousted Italy on penalties and Sweden edged Poland. This depth outpaces CONMEBOL's six South American contenders—defending champions Argentina atop qualifiers alongside Ecuador, Colombia, Uruguay, and Brazil—holding steady at 20.5% amid their consistent top-six finish. Africa's nine slots feature rising Morocco (No. 8), but limited elite depth caps them at 3.5%, while Asia's eight (Japan, South Korea) and host North America's trio (USA, Mexico, Canada) trail due to historical knockout struggles, with Oceania's New Zealand negligible post-qualifiers wrap in early April.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices hover around $2.76 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) as of May 8, 2026, pressured by elevated storage levels and robust production amid mild weather. The latest EIA weekly report for the week ending May 1 showed a +63 Bcf injection to 2,205 Bcf total inventories—surpassing the five-year average and consensus forecasts of +72 Bcf—which has reinforced bearish trader sentiment during the ongoing injection season. Dry gas output averages 110 Bcf per day, supported by steady LNG export demand near records, while near-normal temperatures through late May limit cooling needs. Key catalysts ahead include the May 15 EIA storage release and extended weather outlooks, with summer power burn as a potential upside risk.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's May 2 announcement to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months—prompted by European allies' denial of flyover rights during recent US military operations—has intensified strains within NATO, fueling speculation of reduced US commitment under President Trump. This move fulfills prior threats amid criticisms of uneven burden-sharing, yet trader consensus reflects low implied probabilities for full withdrawal, constrained by the 2024 NDAA's Section 1250A requiring congressional approval beyond the president's unilateral authority under NATO's Article 13. NATO leaders are seeking clarification, with alliance unity and potential deeper cuts in Spain or Italy as key watchpoints ahead of year-end deadlines.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie leads year-to-date at $922 million worldwide but is tapering post its April release, dropping its odds to 5.7%. Avengers: Doomsday holds 21.5% on explosive long-lead metrics and industry polls favoring it overall, though its December slot trails the summer frontrunner. Key catalysts include Spider-Man's presales, Toy Story 5 competition, and review embargoes ahead of the July 31 premiere.

markets.question: Will François Hollande win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Édouard Philippe's official launch of his 2027 presidential campaign on May 10, amid polls showing him narrowly ahead of Jordan Bardella in potential runoffs (e.g., 51.5%-48.5% in recent Elabe), underscores the tight trader consensus atop the market, with Bardella's 34-38% first-round lead in April-May surveys like Harris Interactive and Toluna reflecting National Rally strength post-March municipal elections. A record 30-plus hopefuls fragment the center-right (Retailleau, Attal) and left (Mélenchon, Glucksmann), preventing dominance and mirroring voter desire for change on economy and security. Consolidation via party primaries, Marine Le Pen's July embezzlement trial outcome, or economic shifts could separate frontrunners before April 2027 voting.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Republican victory at 83.5% in the TX-38 House race, reflecting the district's Solid Republican rating from Cook Political Report and others, anchored by Donald Trump's 60.5% 2024 presidential margin in this Houston-area seat opened by incumbent Wesley Hunt's U.S. Senate bid. Democrat Melissa McDonough secured her March 3 primary nomination with 51.6% but trails in fundraising, raising under $50,000 against GOP contenders' millions. Recent March primaries advanced Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck (47.7%) and airport president Shelly deZevallos (18.6%) to the May 26 Republican runoff, where Bonck leads as heavy favorite per analysts, further solidifying GOP path to November 3 general election win amid absent Democratic momentum.

markets.question: Spread: AD Municipal Liberia (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AD Municipal Liberia" if AD Municipal Liberia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Saprissa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on unafut.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Saprissa vs. AD Municipal Liberia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Deportivo Saprissa, second in the Primera División Clausura standings with 31 points from 17 matches, host AD Municipal Liberia, who sit third on 30 points after a stunning 2-1 upset victory at Estadio Ricardo Saprissa Aymá on April 12—Liberia's first win there in 23 head-to-head meetings, ending Saprissa's unbeaten streak despite their prior 5-1 cup rout on March 29. Saprissa's recent form shows prolific scoring (10 goals in last five), but defensive lapses allowed late concessions, boosting Liberia's confidence amid the tight title race. No major injuries reported from official team updates; home advantage and historical dominance (19 wins, 7 draws, 2 losses overall) favor Saprissa rebound, though Liberia's away resilience adds upset potential for props like total goals (averaging 3.21 H2H).

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors Anthropic at 95.6% implied probability for the best coding AI model by May 31, driven by Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7 variants dominating leaderboards like SWE-Bench (93.9% for Mythos Preview), LiveCodeBench, and SonarSource code quality rankings, where Anthropic models occupy multiple top spots. Recent hype around Claude Code—a neurosymbolic-enhanced system enabling engineers to ship dozens of features autonomously—has solidified its edge in complex code generation, agentic workflows, and software engineering tasks, with no comparable breakthroughs from rivals in the past month. A surprise pre-deadline release or benchmark-topping update from OpenAI, Google, or DeepSeek could challenge this, though historical timelines make it unlikely absent leaks.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Emmanuel Macron term-limited after two consecutive terms, France's 2027 presidential election—first round expected in April—features a crowded, fragmented field of declared and potential candidates amid ongoing parliamentary instability from the 2024 hung National Assembly. Recent catalysts include Édouard Philippe's campaign launch this week, positioning him as the top centrist to challenge far-right frontrunners Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen, who lead polls; Jean-Luc Mélenchon's announcement of a fourth left-wing bid; and Bruno Retailleau's designation by Les Républicains. March 2026 municipal elections bolstered far-right gains and left-wing urban holds, aiding signature collection for the 500 parrainages required for ballot access by early 2027. Trader consensus reflects early polling dominance by National Rally, but unity challenges on left and center could shift dynamics ahead of potential party negotiations.

markets.question: Will Delcy Rodríguez be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

March 2026 CPI rose 3.4% month-on-month—the highest of the year—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9% and prompting central bank REM survey analysts to revise full-year forecasts upward to 30.5% median from prior sub-25% estimates, driven by regulated utilities, transportation, and education costs. Core inflation held at 34% year-on-year, underscoring persistent pressures despite primary fiscal surpluses. Key differentiator: April CPI data, due mid-May, could validate convergence near 30% or signal further upside if exceeding 2.6% consensus.

markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold June 2026 futures (GC) hover near $4,730 per ounce, with trader sentiment balancing persistent U.S. inflation pressures against safe-haven demand. Critical catalysts include upcoming May CPI and PPI releases this week, plus the June FOMC meeting, where policy signals could pivot market-implied rate paths and gold's end-June positioning.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Spread: Rangers FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Scottish Premiership game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Rangers FC" if Rangers FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Hibernian FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on spfl.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Rangers FC vs. Hibernian FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Scottish Premiership game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rangers host Hibernian at Ibrox in the Scottish Premiership Championship Round on May 13, amid a fiercely contested title race where Hearts lead with 70 points from 33 games, Rangers sit second on 69, and Celtic trail on 67. Rangers' trader consensus reflects urgency after a shocking 2-3 home loss to Motherwell on April 26—snapping momentum from their 6-3 thrashing of Falkirk—while Hibs, fifth in the table, fell 1-2 to Hearts that weekend. Rangers boast an unbeaten run in recent head-to-heads (three wins, one draw since October), strong Ibrox form, but face injury concerns with Ryan Naderi sidelined long-term and Tuur Rommens doubtful; Hibs monitor Josh Mulligan's ankle recovery. Late-season stakes amplify pressure for the hosts.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.
